description: Port CCEM APM from Python to Phoenix/Elixir with AG-UI, A2UI, Swift WebKit, LiveView+D3.js Ralph flowchart

userStories.title: Scaffold Phoenix project with deps and supervision tree
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Phoenix project scaffolded at ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4 with all required dependencies so that subsequent user stories have a working foundation to build on. This includes mix phx.new, adding Phoenix, LiveView, Jason, and Bandit to mix.exs, configuring the supervision tree with Application module, and verifying the app compiles and boots cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Phoenix project created at ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4 via mix phx.new apm_v4 --no-ecto (Ecto added later if needed)
mix.exs includes deps: {:phoenix, '~> 1.7'}, {:phoenix_live_view, '~> 1.0'}, {:jason, '~> 1.4'}, {:bandit, '~> 1.5'}
Application module defines a supervision tree with Endpoint and PubSub children
mix deps.get succeeds without errors
mix compile succeeds without warnings
mix phx.server starts and serves on port 3031
Config sets port 3031 to match existing APM convention
userStories.notes: Phoenix 1.8.3 scaffolded with Elixir 1.19.5/OTP 28. Port 3031 configured. All deps installed. 5/5 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Port agent registry and session management to ETS/GenServer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Python dict-based AGENTS registry, notification system, and session state ported to Elixir using ETS tables and a GenServer so that agent fleet state is managed concurrently and survives process restarts. The Python source uses global dicts (AGENTS, TASKS, PLANE_PM, SLASH_COMMANDS) and threading locks which map naturally to ETS + GenServer.
acceptanceCriteria: ApmV4.AgentRegistry GenServer created with ETS-backed storage
register_agent/2 accepts agent_id and metadata map, stores in ETS
get_agent/1 retrieves agent by ID, list_agents/0 returns all agents
update_status/2 updates agent status (idle, active, error, discovered)
Session tracking: register_session/1, get_session/1, list_sessions/0
Notification queue ported: add_notification/1, get_notifications/0, clear_notifications/0
GenServer starts under the application supervision tree
mix test passes for AgentRegistry with at least 6 test cases
userStories.notes: GenServer with 3 ETS tables (agents, sessions, notifications). 14 test cases covering agent CRUD, session tracking, notification queue with 200-cap, and error handling. 19/19 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Port HTTP API endpoints to Phoenix controllers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Python HTTPServer endpoints ported to Phoenix controllers so that Claude Code hooks and external tools can register agents, send heartbeats, and query status via the same API contract. The Python server handles GET /api/status, POST /api/register, POST /api/heartbeat, GET /api/agents, and POST /api/notify.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/status returns JSON with uptime, agent_count, session_id, server_version
POST /api/register accepts {agent_id, name, tier, status, deps, metadata} and registers agent
POST /api/heartbeat accepts {agent_id, status} and updates agent last_seen timestamp
GET /api/agents returns JSON array of all registered agents with their metadata
POST /api/notify accepts {title, message, level} and adds to notification queue
All endpoints return proper JSON Content-Type headers
Error responses return appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 404, 500)
mix test passes for all controller actions with at least 10 test cases
userStories.notes: Phoenix API controller with 5 endpoints: GET /api/status, POST /api/register, POST /api/heartbeat, GET /api/agents, POST /api/notify. Proper HTTP status codes (201 for register, 400 for missing fields, 404 for not found). 16 test cases covering happy paths, error handling, and content-type validation. Also added clear_all/0 to AgentRegistry for safe test isolation. 35/35 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Create LiveView dashboard layout with daisyUI/Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I need the embedded HTML/CSS dashboard from the Python APM ported to a Phoenix LiveView with daisyUI components so that I have a modern, responsive monitoring interface. The Python version embeds a full HTML dashboard with sidebar navigation, agent cards grid, notification area, and session info panel.
acceptanceCriteria: LiveView module ApmV4Web.DashboardLive mounted at root path /
daisyUI and Tailwind CSS installed and configured in assets pipeline
Sidebar navigation with links: Dashboard, Agents, Ralph, Sessions, Settings
Main content area with agent cards grid showing name, status badge, tier, last_seen
Top bar with project name, uptime counter, agent count summary
Notification toast area in top-right corner
Responsive layout that works at 1024px and above
Dark theme applied matching the existing APM dashboard aesthetic
LiveView mounts and renders without JavaScript errors
userStories.notes: DashboardLive LiveView at / with daisyUI/Tailwind. Sidebar nav (Dashboard, Agents, Ralph, Sessions, Settings), top bar with agent count badge + LIVE indicator + clock JS hook + notification bell dropdown + theme toggle. Stats grid (6 cards: Agents, Active, Idle, Errors, Sessions, Notifications). Agent fleet grid with tier badges, status badges, last_seen formatting. Right panel with 4 tabs (Inspector, Ralph, Commands, TODOs). D3 graph placeholder. Dark theme default. Responsive layout (lg:grid-cols-6). 11 new LiveView test cases. 45/45 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Implement D3.js dependency graph as LiveView JS hook
userStories.description: As a user, I need the D3.js force-directed dependency graph from the Python APM dashboard ported to a Phoenix LiveView JavaScript hook so that I can visualize agent relationships and dependencies interactively. The Python version embeds D3.js code that renders agents as nodes with dependency edges.
acceptanceCriteria: LiveView JS hook 'DependencyGraph' created in assets/js/hooks/dependency_graph.js
D3.js force simulation renders agents as circle nodes with labels
Dependency edges drawn as lines between related agents
Nodes color-coded by status: green=active, gray=idle, red=error, blue=discovered
Nodes draggable with force simulation physics
Graph updates when agent data changes via pushEvent from LiveView
Tooltip on hover showing agent details (name, tier, status, deps)
Graph renders correctly with 0, 1, 5, and 20+ agents
userStories.notes: D3.js v7 added to vendor. DependencyGraph LiveView JS hook at assets/js/hooks/dependency_graph.js with force simulation, draggable nodes, status color-coding (green=active, gray=idle, red=error, blue=discovered), directional arrow edges, hover tooltips showing agent details, click-to-inspect via select_agent event. phx-update=ignore on container. push_graph_data/2 builds edges from agent deps and pushes agents_updated event. Inspector tab shows selected agent details. 9 new test cases (graph container, hook attribute, empty state, multi-agent, 5+ agents, select_agent, unknown agent, CSS sizing). 52/52 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Implement AG-UI event streaming endpoint
userStories.description: As an AG-UI client, I need a Server-Sent Events endpoint that streams agent lifecycle events per the CopilotKit AG-UI protocol so that external UIs and CopilotKit-compatible clients can subscribe to real-time agent activity. Events follow the AG-UI spec: TEXT_MESSAGE_START, TEXT_MESSAGE_CONTENT, TEXT_MESSAGE_END, TOOL_CALL_START, TOOL_CALL_ARGS, TOOL_CALL_END, STATE_SNAPSHOT, RUN_STARTED, RUN_FINISHED.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/ag-ui/events returns text/event-stream Content-Type
SSE connection stays open and streams events as they occur
Events formatted as AG-UI protocol: {type, data, timestamp, runId, threadId}
TEXT_MESSAGE_START/CONTENT/END events emitted when agents produce output
TOOL_CALL_START/ARGS/END events emitted when agents invoke tools
STATE_SNAPSHOT event emitted periodically with full agent fleet state
RUN_STARTED/RUN_FINISHED events emitted on agent registration/completion
Each event includes monotonically increasing sequence number
Client can filter events by agent_id via query parameter
userStories.notes: ApmV4.EventStream GenServer with sequence counter, event storage (500 cap), and PubSub broadcasting. SSE controller at GET /api/ag-ui/events with text/event-stream Content-Type, chunked transfer encoding, keepalive comments every 30s. Initial STATE_SNAPSHOT on connect with full fleet state. Agent_id query param filtering. AgentRegistry emits RUN_STARTED on register_agent and RUN_FINISHED on completed/finished status transitions. All 9 AG-UI event types supported: TEXT_MESSAGE_START/CONTENT/END, TOOL_CALL_START/ARGS/END, STATE_SNAPSHOT, RUN_STARTED, RUN_FINISHED. Monotonically increasing sequence numbers. 28 new test cases (16 EventStream + 12 controller). 80/80 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Implement A2UI declarative component rendering endpoint
userStories.description: As an A2UI client, I need a JSONL streaming endpoint that sends declarative UI component specifications per the Google A2UI protocol so that any compatible renderer (web, mobile, desktop) can display APM dashboard components without custom UI code. Components include cards, charts, tables, and alert specifications.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/a2ui/components returns application/jsonl Content-Type
Each line is a valid JSON object describing a UI component
Component types supported: card, chart, table, alert, badge, progress
Card components include title, body, footer, variant fields
Chart components include type (bar, line, pie), data, labels fields
Table components include columns, rows, sortable fields
Alert components include level (info, warning, error), message, dismissible fields
Components include unique IDs for incremental updates
Endpoint accepts Accept header to negotiate between JSONL stream and single JSON response
userStories.notes: A2UI JSONL streaming endpoint at GET /api/a2ui/components with content negotiation (application/jsonl default, application/json via Accept header). ComponentBuilder module generates 6 component types: card (4 stat cards with title/body/footer/variant), chart (pie chart of status distribution with chart_type/data/labels), table (agents table with columns/rows/sortable), alert (from notifications with level/message/dismissible), badge (per-agent status with label/value/variant), progress (tier distribution with value/max/percentage). Custom MIME type registered in config.exs. Flexible API pipeline for jsonl+json Accept headers. Unique IDs on all components for incremental updates. 33 new test cases (21 ComponentBuilder + 12 controller). 113/113 tests pass. Zero compiler warnings.

userStories.title: Rebuild Ralph flowchart as LiveView + D3.js
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Ralph methodology flowchart rebuilt from the React/xyflow implementation at ~/Developer/ralph/flowchart/ into a LiveView page with D3.js so that the step-by-step Ralph progression is visible in the APM dashboard. The original uses ReactFlow with custom node components, animated edges, and step highlighting.
acceptanceCriteria: LiveView module ApmV4Web.RalphFlowchartLive mounted at /ralph
D3.js renders Ralph methodology steps as custom-styled rectangular nodes
Edges between steps rendered as animated SVG paths with directional arrows
Node styles match original: distinct colors per phase (planning=blue, execution=green, review=orange)
Current active step highlighted with glow effect and bold border
Step progression controllable via LiveView: advance_step, reset, jump_to_step
Clicking a node shows step details in a sidebar panel
Layout algorithm positions nodes in a top-to-bottom flowchart arrangement
Flowchart renders correctly with all Ralph methodology phases

userStories.title: Add PubSub real-time updates for LiveView clients
userStories.description: As a user viewing the dashboard, I need real-time updates pushed to my browser when agent state changes so that the dashboard reflects current fleet status without manual refresh. Phoenix.PubSub should broadcast agent registrations, heartbeats, status changes, and notifications to all connected LiveView clients.
acceptanceCriteria: Phoenix.PubSub configured with topic 'apm:agents' for agent state changes
Phoenix.PubSub configured with topic 'apm:notifications' for notification broadcasts
AgentRegistry broadcasts on register, update_status, and heartbeat
DashboardLive subscribes to PubSub topics on mount
Agent cards update in real-time when status changes (no page refresh)
New agent registrations appear in the grid within 1 second
Notification toasts appear in real-time when POST /api/notify is called
D3.js dependency graph re-renders when agent topology changes
Multiple browser tabs all receive updates simultaneously

userStories.title: Implement session JSONL parser for metrics extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Python session log parsing logic ported to Elixir so that the dashboard can display token usage, tool call counts, and session duration metrics. The Python version reads .jsonl files from ~/.claude/projects/ and extracts token counts, tool invocations, and conversation turns.
acceptanceCriteria: ApmV4.SessionParser module created with parse_jsonl/1 accepting a file path
Extracts total input_tokens and output_tokens from assistant messages
Counts tool_use blocks by tool name (Read, Write, Bash, Edit, etc.)
Calculates session duration from first to last message timestamp
Counts total conversation turns (user messages)
Returns structured map: %{tokens: %{input, output}, tools: %{}, duration_seconds, turns}
Handles malformed JSONL lines gracefully (skip and log warning)
Handles missing or empty files gracefully (return zero-value defaults)
mix test passes with at least 5 test cases including edge cases

userStories.title: Build Swift WebKit wrapper app for macOS
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I need a native Swift/AppKit application that wraps the APM dashboard in a WKWebView window so that I can run the monitor as a standalone desktop app with proper window management, dock icon, and native feel instead of a browser tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Swift project created at ~/Developer/ccem/apm-v4/swift-wrapper/ with Package.swift or Xcode project
AppKit application with a single NSWindow containing a WKWebView
WKWebView loads http://localhost:3031 on launch
Window title set to 'CCEM APM v4' with appropriate size (1200x800 default)
Window remembers position and size between launches (NSUserDefaults)
Menu bar includes Reload (Cmd+R) and Open in Browser (Cmd+Shift+B) items
App displays a connection error view if localhost:3031 is not reachable
App builds with swift build or xcodebuild without errors
App icon placeholder included in Assets.xcassets

userStories.title: Integration testing across all components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive integration tests verifying that all ported components work together end-to-end so that I can confidently deploy the Phoenix APM as a drop-in replacement for the Python version. Tests should cover HTTP API, LiveView mounting, D3.js rendering, AG-UI streaming, A2UI components, and PubSub broadcasting.
acceptanceCriteria: mix test runs all integration tests in test/integration/ directory
API tests: register agent via POST, verify via GET /api/agents, send heartbeat, check status
LiveView tests: DashboardLive mounts and renders agent cards
LiveView tests: RalphFlowchartLive mounts and renders flowchart container
SSE test: connect to /api/ag-ui/events, register agent, verify RUN_STARTED event received
A2UI test: GET /api/a2ui/components returns valid JSONL with expected component types
PubSub test: register agent via API, verify LiveView receives update within 2 seconds
Session parser test: parse a sample .jsonl fixture and verify extracted metrics
All tests pass with mix test --include integration
Zero compiler warnings on mix compile --warnings-as-errors
